merger_name: Apollo Capital Management – Questex Holdings Group
merger_description:
Funds managed or advised by affiliates of Apollo Capital Management, L.P., a wholly-owned indirect subsidiary of Apollo Global Management, Inc. (**AGM**, together with the indirect subsidiaries of AGM managing the investment funds, **Apollo**) propose to indirectly acquire 100% of the outstanding stock of Questex Holdings Group, Inc. (**Questex**) (the **Acquisition**).

Apollo is a high-growth, global alternative asset manager organised under US law and headquartered in New York. It provides capital solutions to businesses through a fully integrated investment platform spanning investment grade to private equity. As of 31 March 2026, Apollo had approximately USD$1.03 trillion of assets under management. Examples of current investments include, inter alia, companies in education, insurance, financial services, and leisure businesses.

Questex is a privately-held, US-based business-to-business (**B2B**) events and information services company with subsidiaries in Hong Kong, Singapore, the United Kingdom, and the United States.

Questex is principally active in the organisation of B2B events. B2B events are organized gatherings – including trade shows, trade fairs, exhibitions, conferences, and congresses – at which businesses come together to showcase products and services, meet potential customers and suppliers, exchange industry knowledge, and conduct commercial transactions.

Questex operates across three key industry verticals:

* **Life Sciences, Health, and Wellness:** events and digital properties serving pharma, biotech, research, medical devices, beauty, fitness professionals and more (e.g., Fierce Pharma, Fierce Biotech, International Esthetics, Cosmetics & Spa Conference).
* **Living:** events serving residential, operational real estate, and destination sectors (e.g., International Hospitality Investment Forum, The Hospitality Show, Bar & Restaurant Expo).
* **Entertainment and Infrastructure:** events for media, live production, and connectivity (e.g., Sensors Converge, LDI, StreamTV).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Funds managed or advised by affiliates of Apollo Capital
Management, L.P., a wholly-owned indirect subsidiary of Apollo
Global Management, Inc. (AGM, together with the indirect
subsidiaries of AGM managing the investment funds, Apollo),
proposes to indirectly acquire 100% of the outstanding stock of
Questex Holdings Group, Inc. (Questex), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).
Apollo Capital Management, L.P. applied for a notification waiver in
respect of the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Apollo, is a high-growth, global alternative asset
manager organised under US law and headquartered in New York. It
provides capital solutions to businesses through a fully integrated
investment platform spanning investment grade to private equity. As
of 31 March 2026, Apollo had approximately USD$1.03 trillion of
assets under management. Examples of current investments
include, inter alia, companies in education, insurance, financial
services, and leisure businesses.
The target, Questex, is a privately-held US-based business-to-
business (B2B) events and information services company with
subsidiaries in Hong Kong, Singapore, the United Kingdom, and the
United States. It is principally active in the organisation of B2B
events, including trade shows, trade fairs, exhibitions, conferences,
and congresses. It does not host any events in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no overlap between Apollo, its connected entities
and Questex in the supply of B2B event organisation
services in Australia
b. Questex has a very small presence in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
